video.title: Adam Yates serves up the PAIN | Volta a Catalunya Stage 3 2021

transcript.clean_text: welcome back to the lantern rouge youtube channel to highlights of stage three the voltaire catalonia 2021 the first mountain stage it started at sea level at canal olympic catalonia 188 kilometer stage finishing with the climb to valtter at 2150 meters the first 10ks of the climb are not particularly steep the first five are pretty much false flat but the climb proper is eleven point eight k's at seven and a half percent with two sustained steep sections with eight and five kilometers remaining in the climb for almeida narrowly in the leaders jersey ahead of port and yates it'd be a matter of conserving its losses on this mountain top finish breakaway went up the road with not too many threatening names the best climber in that group clearly being taiman arizona the dsm writer you can see in the background but that break actually became a bit of a problem for the peloton getting a gap of over 10 minutes with jumbo visma to kernick quickstep and ineos pacing to bring it back they brought it back to about five minutes with 45ks remaining and i just don't agree with jumbo bismuth kind of quick step helping out any loss to pace adam yes was the favorite for the stage because if they catch the break even if best case he didn't gap almeida or chrysler on the climb he'd still beat them to the bonus seconds so helping ineos to pace back a really large gap when they only have rho dennis and castro viejo to work on the flat didn't really make sense to me i think they really played into ineos hands in this stage by helping them out and so going into the base of the climb where the false flat section starts with 20 k's to go the breakaway only had about 3 minutes and 45 seconds on the peloton that would not be enough given how hard this finish is the gc team started lining up at the front almost like they're going into a sprint peter sagan bringing up wilco kelderman on the left hand side grant thomas third wheel and he also were able to put a fresh row and dennis on the front as the gradient kicked up a bit we know from the giro last year what he can do to the bunch mark celeste solaire somewhat surprisingly dropping immediately with over 10 k's to go and with alejandra valverde attacking with over eight k's to go on this climb on the early slopes marked by richard carapaz and you may be wondering why is karapas marking valverde how did ineos make that decision that he would mark early moves when they've got adam yates right there as well well it's because carapace lost so much time in the time trial he's 52 seconds behind almeida and nearly on the same time as val verde porton yates being so close to almeida don't need to attack early they're the protected gc riders it means that port thomas and yates don't need to pull they can sit on the wheels while other teams have to chase this move whilst carapaz gets to sit on val verde who smoked that move but didn't really pull with val verde it all came back together again time and aaronsman was up the road but valverde wasn't done this is one of the most impressive performances from valverde today i've seen in the last couple of years looked everyone in the eye and attacked again simon yates had tried to attack or pull a little bit when he bridged across to valverde's earlier move but no one responded to him immediately naira quintana seeing his former teammate valverde go up the road attacked out of the group and it's karapaz marking him once again with i think chicone for trex sigur freida on his will and the same principle applies with carapaz bridging across and all these moves cost kara powers today he had to surge multiple times on the early slopes of this climb but it meant that maznada had to pace the last on mystique for our major second wheel with thomas port and yates being able to sit on and we've still got over five kilometers left in this long climb which goes up to over two thousand meters so with the kernig quickstep bringing back that group of four of valverde chicane quintana and karapas adam yeah said thank you very much attacking across before they'd even been caught and valverde was actually able to snap onto his wheel coos was marking adam yates crossfire didn't look good on this stage he lost quite a bit of time so coos was riding 100 for the stage win and to gain time at this point not defensively for christ like you see the problem for almeida now he's got no teammates we've got five k's left on the climb and he's got three idiots riders glued to his wheel two of which are not very far behind him in the general classification and because adam yates is so far ahead of cousin valverde on gc after the time trial putting over 40 seconds into them he was happy to just ride on the front without them pulling and extend the gap to almeida in this instance the group behind was not working too well whereas yates was able to just ride his pace and try and extend that gap as much as possible until koos with over two and a half k's remaining on the climb attacked adam yates who was able to snap onto his wheel easily this wasn't a particularly steep section yates then went back onto the front riding his pace valverde chose at this point not to blow up he dropped off the back of koos and started to ride his own pace coos still on yates wheel but not for long that acceleration i think really cost sep coos they were going up to the snow line over 2000 meters at this point where it should suit him but yates was able to drop him in the last 1500 meters off the wheel and looked like he was riding away to an easy stage win until esteban chavez teleported out of nowhere i know he had attacked it like four and a half k's remaining but we didn't see him on live images for ages and he was seven seconds behind adam yates at this point so a massive move from esteban chavez kathy and harun van hoka were attacking from behind and with the way chavez had closed that gap particularly in the last two kilometers we thought is he right behind adam yates is he about to close on to him he's only 100 meters behind him but actually yates was able to extend that gap out from 6 seconds to 13 seconds once again as they got onto the flatter sections at the end of this climb and for yates he didn't need to attack that early he only had a seven second deficit to almeida he could have attacked in the last two kilometers or just waited for the bonus seconds but if your legs feel good and you're the favor for the stage why not put in as much time as possible taking a big stage win up to valtter adam yates and setting a new climbing record 13 seconds ahead of esteban chavez for bike exchange and alejandro valverde 19 seconds back some of their best climbing performances in a long time those two really good to see from them as well as i think the biggest surprise of the day grant thomas coming forth on the stage with a signature last kilometer attack this bodes really well for ineos for the tour de france this is what thomas was good at during his peak septus however i think will be ruined attacking adam yates so early especially on a flatter section with so much of the climb to go i said it on the preview video i said in the recap video yesterday he attacked too early on jabel her feet he just needed to sit as long as possible on adam yates he wouldn't have just sat up because that would have meant he'd lost the advantage he'd just worked so hard to gain on almeida and the group behind so i think that early bridge from koos following valverde and then trying to attack yates eventually cost him here here's the stage results adam yates first 13 seconds ahead of estimate chavez 19 ahead of val verde then thomas van gogh of cous cartie woodsport and chicony all between 30 and 36 seconds back adam yates goes into the leader's jersey and is in what looks like an almost unassailable position in the general classification for this catalonia 45 seconds out of port 50 seconds ahead of almeida who's only three seconds ahead of grant thomas in fourth but here's today's stage four profile a really hard mountain stage harder than yesterday one would think two hc climbs as well as a category one the second climb the middle climb of the day isn't too threatening 25 and a half case at 4.2 percent but definitely an energy sapper and the last climb is 18 and a half k's at a consistent six and a half percent except for the last kilometer is quite steep and you know who that should suit cepcus in the past he's shown that he has no issue in back back-to-back hard mountain stages look at criterium did open at stage five for example but the problem is there should be a big break today mark solaire losing time bit curious but i'd expect to see three or four mother star riders samitiya solaire etc trying to get into the break today ineos was such a dominant position on gc already i don't think have a big incentive to chase that breakdown unless there's someone close on gc if you like the video like it down below and i'll see you at the stage 4 highlights tomorrow ciao
